goal: wave15-ui-readiness: Ship SITE_DESIGN_v2 Wave 15 (production UI readiness) per docs/UI_READINESS_AUDIT.md. Implement P0 polish backlog only — no new backend features, no Orbita, no newsletter. Focus: visible dates/type labels on cards, brand copy pass (remove Harvest Hub/companion legacy strings from user UI), community loading/empty UX with editorial fallback, demote companion chat chrome on list pages to Ask entry, trust footer on both sites. Both web-io and web-org. Run pnpm turbo build; backend tests must still pass. Update docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md when done. Open ONE draft PR to main.
summary: Jacky's bot: shipping SITE_DESIGN_v2 Wave 15 production UI readiness — dates/type labels on cards, Harvest-Hub/companion brand cleanup, community empty/loading editorial fallback, companion demoted to an Ask entry on list pages, and a trust footer on both .io and .org (P0 polish only)
acceptanceCriteria: All Wave 15 P0 items in docs/UI_READINESS_AUDIT.md are implemented (or explicitly deferred with reason in the PR body): (1) visible published/updated date + content-type labels on home grid + library/knowledge index + community list cards; (2) brand copy pass removing user-visible 'Harvest Hub'/'Harvest companion' strings; (3) community empty/loading editorial fallback (skeleton + curated highlights, no indefinite 'Loading community…'); (4) companion chat chrome demoted to an 'Open Ask' entry on community/knowledge/library list pages; (5) a trust footer (mission one-liner + © year + low-key contact) on both sites
`pnpm turbo build` passes 6/6 (web-io, web-org, backend, combined, shared, content)
`pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` still passes with no regressions (no backend feature changes in this wave)
`rg -i "harvest hub|harvest companion" apps/web-io apps/web-org data/curated` returns 0 user-facing hits
docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md + docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md updated to mark Wave 15 shipped
Exactly ONE draft PR opened to main (not merged, not marked ready)

tasks.name: shared-foundation
tasks.scopedGoal: Make the SHARED, cross-app changes Wave 15 needs in packages/chat-ui ONLY so that apps/web-io and apps/web-org can each branch from you and do their own page-level polish without colliding on the shared companion widget. Two deliverables: (1) BRAND COPY in the shared companion widget — in packages/chat-ui/src/sidebar-chat.tsx the org COPY entry has `title: 'Harvest companion'`; change it to a v2-brand-neutral title 'Community companion' (keep the io title 'Companion' as-is, keep subtitles/placeholders/toggle copy otherwise unchanged). In packages/chat-ui/src/companion-home-entry.tsx the suggested prompt 'What should I read first on Harvest Hub?' must drop the legacy brand — change it to 'What should I read first in the community?' (or similarly neutral). Grep packages/chat-ui for any other user-visible 'Harvest Hub' / 'Harvest companion' strings and neutralize them to the v2 brand ('Community · Knowledge commons' framing). Do NOT touch packages/shared (the agent-entry text there is intentionally left for a later wave and a backend test asserts on it). (2) ADD A REUSABLE 'OPEN ASK' ENTRY STRIP component to packages/chat-ui that list pages will use instead of the full docked chat panel, so the companion can be DEMOTED on index/list pages per docs/UI_READINESS_AUDIT.md item 9. Add packages/chat-ui/src/companion-ask-strip.tsx exporting a presentational, accessible React component (e.g. `CompanionAskStrip`) with props { site: 'io' | 'org'; href?: string; label?: string; description?: string }. It renders a SUBTLE single-row strip/card (NOT a chat session — no fetch, no streaming, no quota) that links to `/ask` (default href '/ask') with a short prompt like 'Have a question? Open Ask →'. Style it with the existing CSS variables/Tailwind conventions used in sidebar-chat.tsx (var(--border), var(--card), var(--accent), font-serif headings, font-light muted text) so it matches the editorial look on both sites. Use a Next-friendly plain <a href> (the package is framework-agnostic; do not import next/link). Export `CompanionAskStrip` from packages/chat-ui/src/index.ts. Keep everything English-only, light-theme-friendly with the existing dark-mode variables. Run `pnpm install` if node_modules is missing, then `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/chat-ui typecheck` (or `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/chat-ui build` if no typecheck script) and ensure it passes. Do NOT touch apps/**, packages/shared/**, packages/content/**, data/**, docs/**.
tasks.brief: Read docs/UI_READINESS_AUDIT.md (items 4 Brand consistency, and 9 Companion/chat chrome) and load .agents/skills/editorial-ui/SKILL.md + .agents/skills/ux-copy/SKILL.md before writing any UI copy. The companion widget lives in the shared @ai-transformation/chat-ui package consumed by BOTH web apps, so brand fixes and the new Ask-entry strip belong here (done once), and the two web workers branch from your branch. CRITICAL: do NOT edit packages/shared/src/index.ts — its agent-entry 'Harvest Hub' text is out of scope for Wave 15 and apps/backend/src/lanes/agent-protocol/agent-protocol.test.ts asserts on it; changing it would break backend tests. The CompanionAskStrip is purely presentational (a styled link to /ask) — it must NOT start a chat session. Keep exports clean and additive so web-io/web-org build against them. English-only UI, light default + dark toggle.
acceptance: packages/chat-ui no longer contains user-visible 'Harvest companion' / 'Harvest Hub' strings (org companion title is now 'Community companion'; home-entry prompt is brand-neutral)
A new presentational CompanionAskStrip component (subtle link to /ask, no chat session) is exported from packages/chat-ui/src/index.ts and styled with the existing CSS-variable/Tailwind conventions
packages/shared is untouched (agent-entry text and the backend agent-protocol test left intact)
chat-ui typecheck/build passes; no files outside packages/chat-ui changed

tasks.name: web-io-ui
tasks.scopedGoal: Implement the Wave 15 P0 production-UI-readiness polish for ai-transformation.io in apps/web-io ONLY (plus data/curated/io-home.json for brand copy), consuming the shared chat-ui changes from your starting branch (the brand-fixed companion + the new CompanionAskStrip exported from @ai-transformation/chat-ui). Deliver the .io slice of the P0 backlog in docs/UI_READINESS_AUDIT.md: (1) DATES + TYPE LABELS — on the home curated grid cards AND the /library index cards, surface a visible published/updated DATE and a CONTENT-TYPE label (e.g. 'Framework', 'Playbook', 'Guide', 'Insight', 'Curated') so cards read as real published content, not a design-system demo. Source dates/types from the existing card data (curated JSON entries and/or @ai-transformation/content metadata); where a date is genuinely unavailable, omit gracefully rather than faking one. Keep the editorial serif + narrow-column look (load editorial-ui skill). (2) BRAND COPY PASS — in data/curated/io-home.json remove the user-visible legacy 'Harvest Hub' strings: the 'Harvest Hub on .org' label and the 'Harvest Hub — field stories…' summary must use the v2 brand (e.g. 'Community on .org' / 'Community · Knowledge commons — field stories, prompts, and practitioner guides'). Grep apps/web-io for any other user-visible 'Harvest Hub'/'Harvest companion' strings and fix them. (3) COMPANION DEMOTION — on list/index pages (e.g. /library and /insights index, and any other index page that currently renders the full docked SidebarChat companion), STOP rendering the full docked chat chrome and instead render the shared CompanionAskStrip (subtle 'Open Ask →' link to /ask). Keep the full companion on the personal cockpit / content reading experiences where it belongs; the goal is to remove the duplicate persistent chat panel from index/list pages only. Inspect apps/web-io/components/site-companion.tsx, content-page-layout.tsx, and the layout/page wiring to decide where the docked companion currently mounts. (4) TRUST FOOTER — upgrade apps/web-io/components/site-chrome.tsx SiteFooter into a trust footer: a one-line mission statement, a © {current year} AI Transformation line, and a low-key contact/agent-entry link, keeping the existing Library/Insights/Agent-entry/Community links. Use a dynamic current year (new Date().getFullYear()), not a hardcoded year. Ensure `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-io build` and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-io typecheck` pass (run `pnpm install` first if node_modules missing). Do NOT touch apps/web-org/**, apps/backend/**, apps/combined/**, packages/**, data/curated/org-*.json.
tasks.brief: READ FIRST: docs/UI_READINESS_AUDIT.md (items 1 dates+type labels, 4 brand, 7 trust footer, 9 companion demotion — the .io column) and docs/SITE_DESIGN_v2.md §2/§3.3/§10 for .io positioning + route map. LOAD .agents/skills/editorial-ui/SKILL.md + .agents/skills/ux-copy/SKILL.md before any UI/copy work (content-first editorial, English-only, light default + dark toggle). You start from the shared-foundation branch which already has the brand-fixed companion and the new CompanionAskStrip export — import it from @ai-transformation/chat-ui (do NOT re-edit packages/**). Key existing files: components/site-chrome.tsx (SiteFooter to upgrade), components/site-companion.tsx (docked SidebarChat), components/content-page-layout.tsx, lib/nav.ts, the home page + curated grid components, app/library + app/insights, data/curated/io-home.json. This is frontend-only — NO backend API changes (prefer existing card metadata for dates/types). Do not add legacy redirects. NO new features. Capture before/after screenshots of the home grid (with dates/type labels), a list page with the demoted Ask strip, and the new trust footer, and reference the artifact path in your handoff. node_modules are often missing on cloud start — run `pnpm install` first.
acceptance: Home curated grid + /library index cards show a visible date + content-type label (gracefully omitted when no date exists); editorial serif/narrow-column look preserved
data/curated/io-home.json + apps/web-io contain no user-visible 'Harvest Hub'/'Harvest companion' strings (v2 brand used instead)
Full docked companion is removed from list/index pages (e.g. /library, /insights index) and replaced with the shared CompanionAskStrip linking to /ask; companion retained on cockpit/reading surfaces
SiteFooter is a trust footer: mission one-liner + © {dynamic year} + low-key contact/agent link, existing nav links retained
`pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-io build` and typecheck pass; only apps/web-io + data/curated/io-home.json changed
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- `pnpm install` if node_modules missing, then `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-io build` + typecheck.
## Automated
- Build + typecheck must pass. `rg -i "harvest hub|harvest companion" apps/web-io data/curated/io-home.json` must return 0 hits. Confirm CompanionAskStrip is imported from @ai-transformation/chat-ui on list pages and the full docked SidebarChat no longer mounts there.
## Manual

- Frontend-only; reuse existing card metadata for dates/types; don't fake dates. English-only, light default + dark toggle. Don't regress logged-out SEO content.

tasks.name: web-org-ui

Source from existing card/object data and the COMMUNITY_TYPE_LABEL helpers; omit a date gracefully when unavailable. (2) COMMUNITY EMPTY/LOADING UX — in the /community page + components/community-highlights.tsx, REPLACE the indefinite 'Loading community…' spinner-only state with a proper skeleton while loading AND, when the live API returns 0 objects, an editorial fallback using the existing COMMUNITY_HIGHLIGHTS curated highlights (lib/community-highlights.ts), clearly labeled (e.g. 'Editor's picks' / 'Featured from the community') rather than an empty/broken-feeling list (audit items 2 and 6). Keep real objects when present. Ensure `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org build` and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org typecheck` pass (run `pnpm install` first if node_modules missing). Do NOT touch apps/web-io/**, apps/backend/**, apps/combined/**, packages/**, data/curated/io-home.json.

tasks.name: integrate-wave15
tasks.scopedGoal: Produce the single integrated Wave 15 deliverable branch and open ONE draft PR to main. You start from the web-org-ui branch (which includes the shared-foundation chat-ui commits as a common ancestor). Merge `orch/wave15-ui-readiness/web-io-ui` into the current branch. Conflicts should be minimal because the two impl branches touch disjoint trees (apps/web-org + data/curated/org-*.json vs apps/web-io + data/curated/io-home.json) over an identical packages/chat-ui common ancestor; if any conflict arises (most likely none, or in shared chat-ui exports), keep BOTH sides' use of the shared CompanionAskStrip + brand-fixed companion and do not drop either app's polish. After merging: run `pnpm install`, then `pnpm turbo build` (must be 6/6: web-io, web-org, backend, combined, shared, content) and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` from repo root; fix any integration-only breakage (import drift, type mismatches in how the two apps consume @ai-transformation/chat-ui). Run `rg -i "harvest hub|harvest companion" apps/web-io apps/web-org data/curated` and confirm 0 user-facing hits (fix any stragglers in the apps/curated trees if found; do NOT edit packages/shared/src/index.ts — that 'Harvest Hub' is intentionally out of scope and a backend test asserts on it). Then update docs to reflect Wave 15 shipped: docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md and docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md (note: P0 production-UI-readiness shipped — visible dates/type labels on home/library/knowledge/community cards, Harvest-Hub/companion brand cleanup in user UI, community skeleton + editorial COMMUNITY_HIGHLIGHTS fallback on empty/error, companion demoted to a CompanionAskStrip /ask entry on list/index pages, and a trust footer on both sites; backend untouched and tests green; Wave 16 = content supply / L12 draft ingest next). Commit, push exactly your branch, and open a DRAFT PR to main titled 'Wave 15: SITE_DESIGN_v2 production UI readiness (P0 polish)' whose body summarizes each P0 item (dates/type labels, brand pass, community empty/loading fallback, companion demotion, trust footer), lists `pnpm turbo build` + backend test results, includes the brand-string grep result, and explicitly lists any P1/P0 item deferred with reason. Do NOT merge the PR or mark it ready. Check for a PR template (.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md) and use it if present.
tasks.brief: This is a merge + integration + docs task (planners do not code, so this worker performs the merge). The two impl branches are disjoint by directory; main risk is divergent consumption of the shared chat-ui CompanionAskStrip/brand commit — reconcile at the type level so `pnpm turbo build` passes for all 6 packages and backend tests stay green. Read the upstream handoffs (pasted into your prompt) to learn each branch's actual deliverable branch and any deviations or deferred items. Keep merge history with both parents. English docs/code. NO backend feature changes this wave (backend should be untouched; tests must still pass). After build+test green + the brand grep is clean, update docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md + docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md, then open the draft PR via the ManagePullRequest flow against main. node_modules are often missing on cloud start — run `pnpm install` first.

tasks.name: verify-wave15
tasks.scopedGoal: Verify the integrated Wave 15 branch against the planner acceptance criteria and docs/UI_READINESS_AUDIT.md Wave 15 P0 backlog. Run `pnpm install`, `pnpm turbo build` (confirm 6/6: web-io, web-org, backend, combined, shared, content), and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` (confirm pass, no regressions). Run `rg -i "harvest hub|harvest companion" apps/web-io apps/web-org data/curated` and confirm 0 user-facing hits. Then confirm with execution + code-inspection evidence that each P0 item is implemented: (1) visible published/updated date + content-type labels on .io home grid + /library index cards and .org home + /knowledge index + community list cards; (2) brand copy pass — no user-visible 'Harvest Hub'/'Harvest companion' in apps/web-io, apps/web-org, or data/curated (org/io home + org-hub-index); the shared companion title is now brand-neutral; (3) community empty/loading UX — /community renders a skeleton while loading and an editorial COMMUNITY_HIGHLIGHTS fallback (labeled 'Editor's picks'/'Featured') when the API returns 0 objects/errors instead of an indefinite 'Loading community…'; (4) companion demotion — the full docked SidebarChat no longer mounts on .io /library + /insights index and .org /community + /knowledge index pages, replaced by the shared CompanionAskStrip linking to /ask; (5) trust footer — both apps' SiteFooter show a mission one-liner + © {dynamic current year} + low-key contact/agent link. Also confirm docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md + docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md were updated to mark Wave 15 shipped and that exactly ONE draft PR to main exists (not merged/ready). Report execution evidence per criterion; do not pattern-match. If the env blocks a live UI check, run the build + backend tests + brand grep + route/component code-inspection (where each app mounts the companion vs the Ask strip, the community fallback path, footer year logic) and mark unit-test-verified or verifier-blocked honestly. Note any P0 item that is missing or only partially done as a finding.
acceptance: Verification section includes execution evidence (pnpm turbo build 6/6 output, backend test output, the brand-string grep result, and per-page code/route inspection) for every integrate-wave15 acceptance criterion and each of the 5 Wave 15 P0 backlog items
